title: OVERCOME /ovêrcãm/ SUPERAR
definition: To succeed over a problem \br irregular \br overcome; overcame; overcome \br /ovêrcãm/; /ovêrkêim/; /ovêrcãm/
example1: She overcomes fear. \br He overcame addiction. \br They have overcome many obstacles.

title: PAINT /pêint/ PINTAR
definition: To apply color \br regular \br paint; painted; painted \br /pêint/; /pêintid/; /pêintid/
example1: I paint landscapes. \br He painted the house. \br She has painted for years.

title: PROMISE /prómis/ PROMETER
definition: To say you will do something \br regular \br promise; promised; promised \br /prómis/; /prómist/; /prómist/
example1: I promise to return. \br He promised a gift. \br She has promised to help.

title: PROVE /prúv/ PROVAR
definition: To show that something is true \br irregular \br prove; proved; proved/proven \br /prúv/; /prúvd/; /prúvd/ or /prúven/
example1: Evidence proves guilt. \br He proved his point. \br The theory has been proven.

title: REGRET /regrét/ ARREPENDER-SE
definition: To feel sorry about something \br regular \br regret; regretted; regretted \br /regrét/; /regrétid/; /regrétid/
example1: I regret my words. \br He regretted the decision. \br She has regretted it ever since.

title: RELEASE /rilíis/ LANÇAR
definition: To set free or make available \br regular \br release; released; released \br /rilíis/; /rilíist/; /rilíist/
example1: The movie releases Friday. \br He released the bird. \br The prisoner has been released.

title: RING /ríng/ TOCAR (sininho)
definition: To make a bell sound or call \br irregular \br ring; rang; rung \br /ríng/; /rêng/; /rãng/
example1: The phone rings. \br He rang the bell. \br She has rung twice.

title: SAIL /sêil/ NAVEGAR
definition: To travel on water by boat \br regular \br sail; sailed; sailed \br /sêil/; /sêild/; /sêild/
example1: Ships sail across the ocean. \br They sailed to the island. \br He has sailed around the world.

title: SINK /sínk/ AFUNDAR
definition: To go down below the surface \br irregular \br sink; sank; sunk \br /sínk/; /sêngk/; /sãnk/
example1: Rocks sink. \br The ship sank. \br The treasure has sunk.

title: SOUND /sáund/ SOAR
definition: To seem or make noise \br regular \br sound; sounded; sounded \br /sáund/; /sáundid/; /sáundid/
example1: That sounds good. \br The alarm sounded. \br The music has sounded strange.

title: SPELL /spél/ SOBRENATURAL
definition: To write or name letters of a word \br regular or irregular \br spell; spelled/spelt; spelled/spelt \br /spél/; /spéld/ or /spélt/; /spéld/ or /spélt/
example1: How do you spell your name? \br He spelled it wrong. \br She has spelt it correctly.

title: SPOIL /spóil/ ESTREGAR
definition: To ruin or make bad \br regular or irregular \br spoil; spoiled/spoilt; spoiled/spoilt \br /spóil/; /spóild/ or /spóilt/; /spóild/ or /spóilt/
example1: Don't spoil the surprise. \br He spoiled the ending. \br The milk has spoilt.

title: STEAL /stíil/ ROUBAR
definition: To take without permission \br irregular \br steal; stole; stolen \br /stíil/; /stôul/; /stôulen/
example1: Thieves steal valuables. \br He stole a car. \br She has stolen my heart.

title: STICK /stík/ COLAR
definition: To attach or become fixed \br irregular \br stick; stuck; stuck \br /stík/; /stãk/; /stãk/
example1: Glue sticks to paper. \br He stuck the poster on the wall. \br The key has stuck in the lock.

title: STRIKE /stráik/ GREVE
definition: To hit or stop work protesting \br irregular \br strike; struck; struck/stricken \br /stráik/; /strãk/; /strãk/ or /stríken/
example1: Lightning strikes trees. \br He struck the table. \br The workers have struck for better pay.

title: SWIM /suím/ NADAR
definition: To move through water \br irregular \br swim; swam; swum \br /suím/; /suêm/; /suãm/
example1: Fish swim. \br He swam across the lake. \br She has swum every day.

title: TREAT /tríit/ TRATAR
definition: To behave towards someone or give medical care \br regular \br treat; treated; treated \br /tríit/; /tríitid/; /tríitid/
example1: Treat others with respect. \br He treated her badly. \br The doctor has treated many patients.

title: TRUST /trãst/ CONFIAR
definition: To believe someone is honest \br regular \br trust; trusted; trusted \br /trãst/; /trãstid/; /trãstid/
example1: I trust my friends. \br She trusted him completely. \br He has never trusted anyone.

title: UNDERGO /ander gôu/ SUBMETER-SE
definition: To experience something unpleasant \br irregular \br undergo; underwent; undergone \br /andergôu/; /anderuént/; /andergón/
example1: He undergoes surgery. \br She underwent chemotherapy. \br They have undergone huge changes.

title: WITHDRAW /uidrôu/ RETIRAR
definition: To take money out of a bank or move back \br irregular \br withdraw; withdrew; withdrawn \br /uidrôu/; /uidrú/; /uidrón/
example1: I withdraw cash. \br He withdrew from the race. \br She has withdrawn her support.
